evidence: {"reason": "Reproduced on the PR compiler (built from this checkout, HEAD 525c8fb). The struct-field form `struct S{int*_Nullable p;}; void mutate(int*_Nullable*_Nonnull pp){*pp=(int*_Nullable)0;} int f(struct S s){ if(s.p){ mutate(&s.p); return *s.p; } return 0; }` compiles with ZERO diagnostics (exit 0), while the local-variable form `int*_Nullable q; ... mutate(&q); return *q;` IS correctly rejected ('nullable pointer cannot be dereferenced'). Root cause verified in source + AST: the call arg for `&s.p` is UnaryOperator'&' over a MemberExpr; getAddressedVarDecl() recurses into the '&' then calls getVarDeclFromExpr(MemberExpr), which has NO MemberExpr arm (BSCNullabilityCheck.cpp:169-183 handles only DeclRefExpr/ImplicitCastExpr/ParenExpr/SafeExpr/BinaryOperator) and returns nullptr, so the reset at VisitCallExpr:698-701 is skipped entirely. Even if it were identified, that reset path only writes CurrStatusVD, never CurrStatusFP, where field narrowing lives (read at getExprPathNullability MemberExpr arm 429-438; narrowed by SetCFGBlocksByExpr 795-808). The narrowing is real: `if(s.p){return *s.p;}` with no escape is accepted (exit 0) while `return *s.p;` with no check is rejected, proving `if(s.p)` drops s.p to NonNull via CurrStatusFP and that narrowing survives the escape. Authoritative manual authority confirms, not refutes: the PR adds rule 3 at line 4430 ('\u628a\u5b83\u7684\u5730\u5740\u4f20\u7ed9\u4e00\u4e2a\u53ef\u4ee5\u901a\u8fc7\u8be5\u5730\u5740\u5199\u56de\u53ef\u7a7a\u6307\u9488\u7684\u51fd\u6570\u53c2\u6570...\u5176\u53ef\u7a7a\u6027\u72b6\u6001\u6062\u590d\u4e3a Nullable') with NO field/struct exclusion, and \u00a73.4.3 explicitly lists `while(s.p)` as a valid null-check, so struct fields are tracked nullable pointers squarely in rule 3's scope. ParamCanNullOutArg is satisfied (same mutate signature caught for locals). Result: an accepted null dereference on realistic by-value-struct input \u2014 a coverage gap in a partial fix, medium severity, not a regression. The finding is concrete, reachable, and empirically verified; holds under the changed manual, not just a superseded skill.", "run_id": 68}
